summary: Iteration 3 (Multi-AI Bot Management V2) fully verified. Backend: 13/13 new iteration-3 tests pass — bots CRUD with super_admin gating, tools/intents/workflows CRUD, chat with bot_code routing (Booking AI answers Deluxe availability with price, Guest Service AI refuses booking and redirects to Booking AI, Guest Service AI creates laundry service_request with tool_result.ok=true and persists to DB), permission gating (a bot with tool_codes=['request_handover'] cannot call create_booking — server rejects with 'tidak diizinkan'), knowledge filtering (bot with knowledge_categories=['promo'] cannot answer airport_pickup KB question). Regression: iteration-2 12/12 pass; iteration-1 23/23 pass (previously reported 2 flaky failures now green in this run). Frontend: sidebar 'AI Management' collapsible with 5 items (nav-ai-bots/tools/intents/workflows/prompt), /ai/bots renders 2 seed bot cards → detail page loads with all 7 tabs (profile/prompt/permissions/workflow/knowledge/intents/guardrail), profile edit persists across reload, /ai/tools + /ai/intents + /ai/workflows show seeded rows/cards, Chat Simulator sim-bot-select lists both bots with headers/counts, switching bot resets message list, Booking AI answers 'Halo kamar Deluxe 24-26 Agustus 2026' with Rp 550.000/malam + fasilitas, Guest Service AI refuses booking as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _load_bot() falls back to 'booking_marketing' when bot_id/bot_code is invalid — this silently hides misconfiguration. Consider returning HTTP 400 when a bot_id is provided but not found.

server.py chat_message stores conv['bot_id']/'bot_code' via in-memory assignment but the DB $set update (line ~693) does NOT include these keys — the bot association is not persisted per-conversation. This limits later analytics per-bot. Suggest adding 'bot_id' and 'bot_code' to the $set payload on first turn.
server.py permission gating uses ai_tool_to_capability dict inline — extract to ai_service.py to keep server.py <1000 LOC (currently 1119).

server.py workflows_update (PATCH) always overwrites all fields with body.model_dump() including default empty steps — non-idempotent for partial updates. Use exclude_unset=True or a separate WorkflowUpdate model like AIBotUpdate.

tools_update / intents_update accept ToolIn/IntentIn which have required 'name' — a genuine PATCH would need a *Update model with all Optional fields. Right now every 'partial' update must resend name.

seed.py DEFAULT bots use bare 'existing_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT' — if a legacy prompt existed with data-specific to the older bot, it may leak generic guardrails into Booking AI. Working as intended per spec but worth documenting.

ai_service.SERVICE_MAP maps 'complaint_ticket' and 'restaurant_order' to None — these tools are exposed via build_dynamic_prompt but SERVICE_MAP does not translate them. Any AI call attempting service_type='complaint' or restaurant order would fail permission gating (empty capability set). Minor: dead permutation.
BotDetail Save button posts full bot object via PATCH — sends all fields on every save (including unchanged prompt). Fine now but noisy in audit trail.
ChatSimulator posts bot_id every turn — good. But if bots API is empty (fresh env), botId stays null and no chat can be sent. Consider a fallback empty-state UI.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (sidebar nested nav, BotList, BotDetail 7 tabs + save/persist, Tools/Intents/Workflows pages, Chat Simulator bot selector + session reset + Booking-AI vs Guest-Service-AI behaviour)

seed_data_creation: Tests create + delete: 1 TEST_ bot, 1 TEST_ tool, 1 TEST_ intent, 1 TEST_ workflow, 2 TEST_ gated/knowledge-scoped bots (all cleaned up via super_admin DELETE). Chat tests may leave a TEST_ conversation + service_request in DB (Budi laundry). Cloudinary is not touched by iteration-3 tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-3 is fully green backend + frontend. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration3.py -v`. Two seeded bots (booking_marketing, guest_service) are created idempotently by seed.py — do NOT delete them. If you PATCH bot.tool_codes to test permission gating, use a TEST_ bot instead of the seeded ones. Frontend BotDetail loads asynchronously — always wait for `[data-testid="bot-save"]` before asserting on tabs. Chat responses may take 5-45s (LLM calls to GPT-5.4-mini). Guest Service bot legitimately refuses bookings even without hitting a tool — verify by reading `reply` text for words like 'tidak', 'booking ai', 'resepsionis' rather than only checking tool_used. Regression note: iteration-1 backend_test.py — previously reported flaky tests (prompt version + booking-via-tool) both passed cleanly this run.
